Happy Path.description: Normal successful operations with valid inputs

Boundary.description: Edge cases, boundary values, Unicode, and zero-value checks

Negative.description: Error conditions, invalid inputs, and malformed requests

Security.description: Authentication, authorization, RBAC, and token lifecycle tests

Query Behavior.description: Filtering, sorting, pagination, and date-range query behavior

State Transition.description: Lifecycle and workflow transition rules between resource states

Reliability.description: Idempotency, upstream failures, and protocol-level behavior

Contract.description: Response schema and structured error payload validation

High.meaning: Cover first: business-critical flow, auth, destructive actions, money, or data integrity

Medium.meaning: Important validation and common negative behavior

testLevels.Unit: Fast, isolated tests for validation and pure business logic
testLevels.Integration: Interaction tests for API contracts, auth, RBAC, database, and service calls
testLevels.E2E: Cross-system workflow tests; keep the set intentionally small

unit.description: Fast, isolated tests for individual components
unit.guideline: Starting heuristic only; adjust to the system under test

integration.guideline: Increase when auth, persistence, or contracts carry most of the risk

e2e.guideline: Keep focused on a few critical user paths rather than broad coverage
